# WI-QUIC-API-0002 - Incursa.Quic Stream Capacity Callback Work Item

## Summary

Promote the stream-capacity callback slice on top of the real connection/runtime seam while keeping the public stream surface honest.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-API-0001
- REQ-QUIC-API-0005
- REQ-QUIC-API-0008
- REQ-QUIC-API-0009

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-API-0002

## Planned Changes

- Add the public `QuicStreamCapacityChangedArgs` type.
- Add `QuicConnectionOptions.StreamCapacityCallback`.
- Wire the initial peer stream-capacity delta from `QuicConnectionRuntime` into `QuicConnection` and listener-accepted connections.
- Add truthful later peer `MAX_STREAMS` growth reporting on the supported active loopback path without turning the callback into a general event pipeline.
- Add truthful real peer stream-close-driven capacity release reporting on the supported active loopback path without turning the callback into a general event pipeline.
- Keep the callback silent when the supported boundary is not reached or the connection is canceled or disposed before peer limits commit.
- Keep `QuicStream` write/completion behavior unchanged unless a truthful minimum expansion becomes necessary.
- Reconcile the public API analyzer file, trace artifacts, and requirement-home tests with the approved subset.

## Out of Scope

- Write-heavy stream-action pipeline.
- Reset and stop-sending parity.
- 0-RTT.
- Key update.
- Interop-runner enablement.

## Verification Plan

Use API-diff, reflection, and focused requirement-home tests to prove the callback surface exists, the initial peer delta is observed once on the supported loopback path, later real MAX_STREAMS growth is observed on the supported active loopback path, real stream-close-driven release is observed on the supported active loopback path, zero-delta and pre-commit cases remain silent, cancellation/disposal stay honest, and the public boundary remains narrow. No write-side expansion is expected for this slice.

## Completion Notes

This item keeps the stream-capacity callback narrow and truthful for the initial delta, later real MAX_STREAMS growth, and real peer stream-close-driven capacity release on the supported loopback path. Reset/stop-sending parity and broader write-heavy stream behavior remain separate slices.
